name: Not-so-Traditional Creamy Coquito
method: All my life, coquito was always at the dinner table during the holidays. Nothing says “The holidays are here” more than a cup of coquito. My family would make these in batches to sell or give away to family and friends. Everyone makes theirs different and unique to them. Coquito means “little coconut,” and it is a holiday drink that originated in Puerto Rico. The coconut-based drink is mixed with rum and spices, similar to eggnog. This is a traditional drink, and with the help of coconut milk, I managed to make it vegan.

In a large pot, place your coconut milk, condensed coconut milk, evaporated coconut milk, dark rum, light rum, nutmeg and vanilla. Whisk until well combined. Add the cinnamon sticks, star anise, cloves and a pinch of salt, then let simmer, keeping an eye on it and stirring occasionally, for about 10 minutes, or until you get a gentle boil. Once it is done, remove from the heat and set aside to cool.
Strain into a glass jar and refrigerate overnight. If the coquito stiffens up, just run the jar under warm water to loosen the coconut milk. Serve cool and enjoy during the holidays. ¡Buen provecho!
